inputs.label: Material Cost per kg
inputs.businessContext: Cost of filament or resin per kilogram. Based on market prices for common 3D printing materials (PLA, ABS, resin).

inputs.label: Material Weight per Part
inputs.businessContext: Weight of the printed part in grams. Determines material consumption.

inputs.label: Print Time
inputs.businessContext: Total print time per part. Affects machine utilization and labor cost.

inputs.label: Machine Cost per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Hourly cost including depreciation, maintenance, and electricity. Typical range $1-$10 for desktop printers.

inputs.label: Labor Cost per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Hourly wage for operator/post-processing. Based on industry average for technicians.

inputs.label: Post-Processing Time
inputs.businessContext: Time for support removal, sanding, curing, etc. Varies by part complexity.

inputs.label: Batch Size
inputs.businessContext: Number of parts printed simultaneously. Larger batches reduce per-part machine setup cost.

inputs.label: Failure Rate
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of prints that fail. Affects effective yield and cost per good part.

inputs.label: Overhead
inputs.businessContext: Indirect costs (facility, admin, etc.) as percentage of direct costs.

thresholds.failureRatePercent > 10: High failure rate significantly increases cost per good part. Consider optimizing print settings.
thresholds.overheadPercent > 50: Overhead seems high relative to direct costs. Review indirect expenses.
thresholds.desiredMarginPercent > 50: Target margin is very high; may not be competitive.

suggestedActions: If failure rate > 10%: Review print settings, calibrate printer, or use higher quality material.
If overhead > 50%: Analyze indirect costs and consider reducing facility or admin expenses.
If margin too low: Increase batch size, reduce print time, or negotiate material costs.

premiumFeatures: PDF export of detailed cost breakdown
